attributes.action: Maintain the tradition of featuring national heroes on all UK banknotes
attributes.additional_details: ​The Bank of England’s plan to replace historical figures with wildlife on future banknotes erodes our national heritage. Sir Winston Churchill, who led the UK through its darkest hour in WWII, is a global symbol of defiance and resilience. By removing him, we diminish the daily reminders of the history that defined our modern freedom. Our currency should honor those who shaped our nation, not just serve as a neutral canvas. We call on the Government to protect this 50-year tradition.
attributes.background: The Bank of England plans to replace historical figures with wildlife on future banknotes. We believe this erases vital parts of our national identity. Sir Winston Churchill, who led the UK through WWII, should remain on the £5 note as a permanent symbol of our history and resilience.

rejection.details:
Bank notes are the responsibility of the Bank of England, which is independent of the Government and Parliament.

You can find out how the Bank decides who will be on bank notes here:
https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/banknotes/banknote-characters
